channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump naming cabinet officials at 'warp speed,' far head of first term pace
item.description: President-elect Trump is far ahead of his pace from eight years ago when it comes to staffing his second administration, and he's making his picks faster than Biden or Obama during their transitions
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Takeaways from the AP's investigation into Osprey safety issues
item.description: The Osprey is back in the air after being grounded for months following a crash last November that killed eight U.S. service members in Japan.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Kevin Vineys And Aaron Kessler, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hurricane debate at state capitol heats up as temperatures cool down
item.description: Cooler temperatures mean hurricane season is almost behind us, but the debate in the state capital over storm recovery is just heating up.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 women tell Ethics panel Matt Gaetz paid them for sex, attorney says
item.description: Leppard said the House Ethics Committee and his clients went one by one through a series of Venmo transactions.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Nancy Mace introduces Capitol bathroom bill restrictions
item.description: Mace is currently working with House leadership to include the bathroom restrictions into a rules package.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Photo-shoot fail: Biden, Trudeau miss traditional photo with world leaders at G20
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — President Joe Biden inadvertently was a missing man in the traditional photo of world leaders at the final Group of 20 summit of his presidency Monday, a photo-shoot fail that U.S. officials attributed to timing.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania Supreme Court upholds exclusion of certain mail-in ballots, in victory for GOP
item.description: The Keystone State's top court reaffirmed that certain mail-in ballots cannot be counted, in a blow to certain Democrat-led counties.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans file 12 Pennsylvania lawsuits in 'aggressive' push to end recount
item.description: The Republican Party confirmed new lawsuits to Fox News, which they say are aimed at protecting GOP Sen.-elect Dave McCormick's victory over outgoing Democrat Sen. Bob Casey.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: All eyes on House Ethics Committee amid controversy over Gaetz report
item.description: All eyes will be locked on the House Ethics Committee this week as it weighs whether to release its report into Matt Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: The 9 GOP senators who could derail Trump’s Cabinet picks
item.description: Trump can afford three defections within the Senate GOP conference and still get his picks confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal workers brace for Trump overhaul of civil service
item.description: President-elect Trump’s plans to reshape the federal workforce would allow him to fire wide swaths of career employees and replace them with political appointees — a move that comes as he has demanded loyalty at every level of government.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Donald Trump Jr. says pushback against Cabinet picks proves they're the disrupters voters wanted
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump Jr. said Sunday that any pushback from the Washington establishment around his father's unconventional choices for Cabinet proves they are just the kind of disruptors that voters are demanding.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: From the Amazon rainforest, Biden declares nobody can reverse US progress on clean energy
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — Speaking from the Amazon rainforest, President Joe Biden declared Sunday that there’s no going back in America’s “clean energy revolution” even as the incoming Trump administration vows to spur fossil fuel production and scale back efforts against climate change.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic AGs rush to form line of defense against Trump
item.description: Democratic attorneys general across the country are readying their legal defenses against the incoming Trump administration, preparing to pounce on potential violations and even take the president-elect to court if he implements controversial policies.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Trump names fossil fuel executive Chris Wright as energy secretary
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has selected Chris Wright, a campaign donor and fossil fuel executive, to serve as Energy secretary after his second administration begins in January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump is creating team of loyalists after conflicts with Cabinet members in 1st term
item.description: In his second term, President-elect Donald Trump is surrounding himself with people who can amplify his message and agree with how he wants to remake the executive branch.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Florida congressional leaders demand answers from FEMA political scandal
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) - Republican and Democratic leaders on Capitol Hill are preparing for a hearing next week with FEMA administrator Deanne Criswell, regarding alleged political discrimination after hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hubbard County election judge faces felony charges for allegedly allowing unregistered voters to cast ballots
item.description: Prosecutors in Hubbard County say Timothy Michael Scouton — who was head judge at a precinct in rural Badoura Township — allowed 11 people to vote even though they had not registered.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Former Detroit Lions player charged over Capitol riot
item.description: A former Detroit Lions linebacker was arrested and charged with allegedly assaulting law enforcement and other offenses over the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot, according to the Department of Justice (DOJ).
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tapping Noem to lead Homeland Security: Reports
item.description: Noem was first floated as a possible running mate for Trump, but her consideration fizzled after the release of her biography, in which she described killing a dog that was “untrainable.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, WGN 9

item.title: Speaker Mike Johnson: Republicans 'ready to deliver' on Trump's agenda
item.description: Congress returns to a changed Washington as President-elect Donald Trump’s hard-right agenda is quickly taking shape, buoyed by eager Republican allies eyeing a full sweep of power on Capitol Hill while Democrats are sorting out what went wrong.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Fox 5

item.title: Will Trump's hush money conviction stand? A judge will rule on the president-elect's immunity claim
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A gut punch for most defendants, Donald Trump turned his criminal conviction into a rallying cry. His supporters put “I’m Voting for the Felon” on T-shirts, hats and lawn signs. “The real verdict is going to be Nov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11
